CREATE TABLE boolean_test (id INT, a BOOLEAN, b BOOLEAN);
INSERT INTO boolean_test VALUES (1, TRUE, FALSE);
INSERT INTO boolean_test VALUES (2, TRUE, TRUE);
INSERT INTO boolean_test VALUES (3, FALSE, TRUE);
INSERT INTO boolean_test VALUES (4, FALSE, FALSE);
SELECT id, a, b, "and"(a, b) AS result FROM boolean_test;
SELECT id FROM boolean_test WHERE "and"(a, b) = TRUE;
SELECT id FROM boolean_test WHERE "and"(a, b) = FALSE;
SELECT CASE WHEN "and"(a, b) THEN 'Both True' ELSE 'Not Both True' END AS status FROM boolean_test;
SELECT id FROM (SELECT id, "and"(a, b) AS result FROM boolean_test) AS subquery WHERE result = TRUE;
CREATE TABLE additional_test (id INT, x BOOLEAN);
INSERT INTO additional_test VALUES (1, TRUE);
INSERT INTO additional_test VALUES (2, FALSE);
SELECT bt.id, bt.a, bt.b, at.x, "and"(bt.a, "and"(bt.b, at.x)) AS final_result FROM boolean_test bt JOIN additional_test at ON bt.id = at.id;
SELECT * FROM (SELECT a, b FROM boolean_test WHERE b IS NOT NULL) AS filtered WHERE "and"(a, b);
SELECT "and"(TRUE, TRUE) AS basic;
SELECT "and"(FALSE, TRUE) AS basic;
SELECT "and"(TRUE, FALSE) AS basic;
SELECT "and"(FALSE, FALSE) AS basic;
SELECT "and"(a, b) AS result FROM (SELECT TRUE AS a, FALSE AS b) AS temp;
SELECT id FROM boolean_test WHERE "and"(a, (SELECT x FROM additional_test WHERE id = 1)) = TRUE;
